strengths: 18+ years of full-stack experience, far exceeding the 6-year bar, with sustained TypeScript/Node.js product work at Optilyz, RIDE Capital, and re:cinq, plus React and MongoDB in production
Hands-on AI agent development right now at re:cinq — building MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows — directly matching the role's rarest requirement

Proven testing and craftsmanship depth: TDD, pairing, trunk-based development at RIDE, authored test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and taught multi-day testing courses — a strong fit for the code-review and quality responsibilities
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience listed; Node.js backend work appears framework-agnostic
Kotlin is absent from the skills list, though Java and broad polyglot experience (C/C++, D, Swift, PHP) suggest fast ramp-up
Vitest/Jest/Playwright are not named, although the extensive testing background (Selenium, TDD, custom test runners) mitigates this

key_factors.factor: Current AI agent / MCP development experience
Deep TypeScript/Node.js full-stack track record at scale
Testing expertise and engineering craftsmanship culture

response.justification: The candidate is an exceptionally strong match for this internal-tooling role: he brings nearly two decades of full-stack delivery, recent and directly relevant TypeScript/Node.js/React/MongoDB product work, and — most distinctively — active professional experience building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, the requirement hardest to hire for. His background spans high-throughput backend systems (millions of requests/sec), cloud and container deployment (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes), and multiple languages including Java, satisfying the polyglot requirement. A career-long emphasis on testability, TDD, and knowledge-sharing (workshops, courses, open-source test libraries) maps cleanly onto the role's code-review, process-improvement, and collaboration expectations. Gaps are narrow and tactical — Nest.js, Kotlin, and Vitest specifically — and his demonstrated pattern of rapidly adopting new stacks across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems makes them low-risk.
